title: Image and video capture architecture for three-dimensional reconstruction
abstract: The present approach relates to an automatic and efficient motion plan for a drone to collect and save a qualified dataset that may be used to improve reconstruction of 3D models using the acquired data. The present architecture provides an automatic image processing context, eliminating low quality images and providing improved image data for point cloud generation and texture mapping.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method for inspecting an asset, comprising: controlling one or more drones to execute a flight plan with respect to the asset, wherein the flight plan initially specifies a set of geometric poses about the asset from which image data is to be acquired; acquiring a first set of image data of the asset at a first resolution at each geometric pose; determining sufficiency of the first set of image data with respect to a three-dimensional (3D) model reconstruction process; based on a determination that the first set of image data is unsuitable for 3D model reconstruction, acquiring one or more additional sets of image data based on one or more problems identified in the first set of image data; determining sufficiency of the first set of image data combined with the one or more additional sets of image data with respect to the three-dimensional (3D) model reconstruction process; and reconstructing a 3D model when the first set of image data combined with the one or more additional sets of image data are determined to be suitable for 3D model reconstruction. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the set of geometric poses comprises locations and orientations of the drones with respect to the asset. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the one or more drones comprise unmanned aerial vehicles, unmanned submersible vehicles, or other autonomously moving vehicles. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein at least a portion of the one or more additional sets of image data are at a higher resolution than the first set of image data. 5. The method of claim 1 , wherein acquiring one or more additional sets of image data based on one or more problems identified in the first set of image data comprises: identifying sub-regions of the asset; updating the flight plan to generate an updated flight plan having a set of supplemental geometric poses for imaging the sub-regions; executing the updated flight plan to acquire the one or more additional sets of image data. 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein acquiring one or more additional sets of image data based on one or more problems identified in the first set of image data comprises: identifying sub-regions of the asset; adjusting one or more camera operational parameters; executing the flight plan or an updated flight plan for image the sub-regions and generate the one or more additional sets of image data using the adjusted camera operational parameters. 7. The method of claim 1 , wherein the sufficiency of the first set of image data and/or the one or more additional sets of image data is determined in view of a 3D model generated using the respective image data. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the sufficiency of the first set of image data and/or the one or more additional sets of image data is determined in view of raw image data. 9. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: identifying one or more problem images in the first set of image data or the one or more additional sets of image data prior to or as part of the sufficiency determination; and filtering out the one or more problem images. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the problem images are images that are underexposed, overexposed, exhibit artifacts, have improper depth of field, or exhibit deformation. 1 1. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: identifying one or more images exhibiting motion effects in the first set of image data or the one or more additional sets of image data prior to or as part of the sufficiency determination; and determining one or both of a motion effect direction or magnitude; and correcting the motion effect based on one or both of the determined direction or magnitude. 12. An image analysis system, comprising: one or more processors configured to execute routines; a memory storing one or more non-tangible, processor executable routines, which, when executed by the one or more processors, cause acts to be performed comprising: controlling one or more drones to execute a flight plan with respect to the asset, wherein the flight plan initially specifies a set of geometric poses about the asset from which image data is to be acquired; 

 acquiring a first set of image data of the asset at a first resolution at each geometric pose; determining sufficiency of the first set of image data with respect to a three-dimensional (3D) model reconstruction process; based on a determination that the first set of images is unsuitable for 3D model reconstruction, identifying sub-regions of the asset corresponding to unsuitable or insufficient image data; acquiring one or more additional sets of image data of the sub-regions by having the drone re- execute the flight plan or an updated flight plan; determining sufficiency of the first set of image data combined with the one or more additional sets of image data with respect to the three-dimensional (3D) model reconstruction process; and reconstructing a 3D model when the first set of image data combined with the one or more additional sets of image data are determined to be suitable for 3D model reconstruction. 13. The image analysis system of claim 12, wherein at least a portion of the one or more additional sets of image data are at a higher resolution than the first set of image data. 14. The image analysis system of claim 12, wherein the one or more non-tangible, processor executable routines, when executed by the one or more processors, cause further acts to be performed comprising: updating the flight plan to generate the updated flight plan, wherein the updated flight plan comprises a set of supplemental geometric poses for imaging the sub-regions. 15. The image analysis system of claim 12, wherein the one or more non-tangible, processor executable routines, when executed by the one or more processors, cause further acts to be performed comprising: adjusting one or more camera operational parameters; acquiring the one or more additional sets of image data of the sub-regions by having the drone re-execute the flight plan or an updated flight plan and generate the one or more additional sets of image data using the adjusted camera operational parameters. 16. The image analysis system of claim 12, wherein the sufficiency of the first set of image data and/or the one or more additional sets of image data is determined in view of a 3D model generated using the respective image data. 17. The image analysis system of claim 12, wherein the sufficiency of the first set of image data and/or the one or more additional sets of image data is determined in view of raw image data. 18. A method for imaging a surface of an asset, comprising: controlling one or more drones to execute a flight plan with respect to the asset, wherein the flight plan initially specifies a set of geometric poses about the asset from which image data is to be acquired; for one or more surfaces of the asset, projecting a visual pattern onto the respective surface to create a temporarily patterned surface; acquiring two or more images of the temporarily patterned surface; and matching features present in the two or more images, wherein at least a portion of the features matched correspond to regions of the projected pattern. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising: generating a point cloud from the matched features; and reconstructing a 3D model based on the point cloud. 20. The method of claim 18, wherein the one or more surfaces on which patterns are projected are relatively featureless within the field of view.
